gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/138)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/138)
text: سَأَلْتُ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ مَتَى يُطَلِّقُ الْغَائِبُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَوْ رَوَى إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَوْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ إِذَا مَضَى لَهُ شَهْرٌ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Razeyn, form Ibn Sama’at who said,

‘I asked Muhammad Bin Abu Hamza, ‘When can the absentee divorce?’ He said, ‘Is’haq Bin ammar narrated to me, or Is’haq Bin Ammar reported from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, or Abu Al-Hasan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When a month passes by for him’.
en.sarwar: 8. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from ibn Sama‘ah who has said the following: “I once asked Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah about when a man absent from his wife can divorce. He said that Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar narrated to him from abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, or abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who said that he can do so after one month.”
